channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Border deployment vehicle accident kills two service members, third in critical condition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two service members deployed to the U.S. Southern border were killed and a third is in serious condition after a vehicle accident near Santa Teresa, New Mexico, the military announced late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon deputy chief of staff is second Hegseth advisor removed amid DOD leak probe
item.description: Pentagon deputy chief of staff Darin Selnick was removed from the Department of Defense headquarters on Tuesday, according to an official who confirmed the matter with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration refers NY AG Letitia James for possible prosecution over allegations of mortgage fraud
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James faces mortgage fraud allegations from property purchases decades ago in which she allegedly listed her father as her husband.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Gov. Shapiro arson attack: Religious bias suspected, DA says
item.description: "We do believe that there was a religious bias involved, and we're going to pursue that in our prosecution," Chardo said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE associate is made acting head of foreign assistance at the State Department, a US official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has expanded the power of adviser Elon Musk's government-cutting team over the State Department, making a Musk lieutenant acting head of foreign assistance. A senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawyers for Harvard in Trump administration dispute are no strangers to high-profile legal matters
item.description: The two attorneys representing Harvard University in a pitched fight with the Trump administration are no strangers to the spotlight or to Washington investigations that reach into the White House.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats prioritizing illegal immigrants over citizens is ‘mind-boggling,’ White House says
item.description: The comments come as Sen. Chris Van Hollen floated possibly traveling to El Salvador to check on a Maryland resident the Trump administration deported to El Salvador in an “administrative error.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC claims Republicans will 'trick' Americans into identity politics, stoking racial divisions
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez told rally goers Monday evening not to let Republicans "trick" them into thinking they are defined by their race or other immutable characteristics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: “Threatening to harm public officials is a criminal act that cannot be excused as political discourse,” acting U.S. Attorney Richard Moultrie, Jr. said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump plans order to cut funding for NPR and PBS
item.description: President Trump and GOP members of Congress have accused the public broadcasters of biased and "woke" programming. The president plans a rescission, which would give Congress 45 days to approve the directive or allow funding to be restored.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik and Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: GOP hits back after judges demand Trump allies be condemned for targeting judiciary
item.description: Sen. Cynthia Lummis led a letter from Wyoming's congressional delegation slamming condemnations from more than 100 Cowboy State jurists for inaction against Trump's "attacks" on judges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Trump agency reveals key reason why REAL ID will be enforced
item.description: In a memo exclusively obtained by Fox News Digital, the department said that part of the reason REAL ID will be enforced is to prevent those in the country illegally from flying domestically.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump directs agencies to follow Supreme Court rulings, as he continues to rein in administrative state
item.description: Experts were optimistic on a new Trump White House memo directing three-letter agencies to abide by Supreme Court rulings – however, how the memo will be enforced remains in question.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Translating what Kennedy's anti-vaccine allies hear in his response to the measles outbreak
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — When the nation’s top health official, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., endorsed the measles vaccine this month after an outbreak in Texas claimed the life of a second child, his comments made waves because he has spent 20 years making false claims that vaccines are unsafe.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Core Democratic groups are preparing to be targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As President Donald Trump pushes the historical boundaries of executive power, some of the Democratic Party's core political institutions are preparing for the possibility that the federal government may soon launch criminal investigations against them.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: As more details emerge, experts said they’ll be watching for responses from top political figures on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump pharma tariff threat raises specter of shortages, price hikes
item.description: The pharmaceutical industry is bracing for chaos if President Trump follows through on his threat to impose "a major tariff" on prescription drug imports.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Maher raves about dinner with Trump, Geraldo Rivera weighs in
item.description: Rivera was criticized when he appeared on “Real Time with Bill Maher” and said the president can be friendly.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence. It began gaining momentum after the Supreme Court’s 2022 ruling overturning Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
